2604.00921.title: Representation Selection via Cross-Model Agreement using Canonical Correlation Analysis
2604.00921.paper_summary: Modern vision pipelines increasingly rely on pretrained image encoders whose representations are reused across tasks and models, yet these representations are often overcomplete and model-specific. We propose a simple, training-free method to improve the efficiency of image representations via a post-hoc canonical correlation analysis (CCA) operator. By leveraging the shared structure between representations produced by two pre-trained image encoders, our method finds linear projections that serve as a principled form of representation selection and dimensionality reduction, retaining shared semantic content while discarding redundant dimensions. Unlike standard dimensionality reduction techniques such as PCA, which operate on a single embedding space, our approach leverages cross-model agreement to guide representation distillation and refinement. The technique allows representations to be reduced by more than 75% in dimensionality with improved downstream performance, or enhanced at fixed dimensionality via post-hoc representation transfer from larger or fine-tuned models. Empirical results on ImageNet-1k, CIFAR-100, MNIST, and additional benchmarks show consistent improvements over both baseline and PCA-projected representations, with accuracy gains of up to 12.6%.
2604.00921.paper_summary_zh: 現代視覺管道越來越依賴預訓練的圖像編碼器，其表示在任務和模型之間重複使用，然而這些表示通常是過度完整且特定於模型的。我們提出了一種簡單的、無需訓練的方法，通過事後的典型相關分析（CCA）運算子來提高圖像表示的效率。通過利用兩個預訓練圖像編碼器產生的表示之間的共享結構，我們的方法找到線性投影，作為一種原則性的表示選擇和降維形式，保留共享的語義內容，同時丟棄冗餘的維度。與標準的降維技術如PCA不同，後者在單一嵌入空間上運作，我們的方法利用跨模型的一致性來引導表示的提煉和精煉。該技術允許表示的維度減少超過75%，同時提高下游性能，或通過從更大或微調模型的事後表示轉移來在固定維度下增強表示。對ImageNet-1k、CIFAR-100、MNIST和其他基準的實證結果顯示，相較於基線和PCA投影表示，均有一致的改善，準確率提升高達12.6%。
2604.00921.author: Dylan B. Lewis et.al.
2604.00921.authors: Dylan B. Lewis, Jens Gregor, Hector Santos-Villalobos
